schema_version.description: Schema version. v1 only; future versions are rejected so older code fails closed against newer configs (a forward-compat upgrade requires a new code release that recognizes the new const).

completed_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when migrate.py --apply completed against this repo. Sole writer is migrate.py itself; SKILL.md wrappers must never set this independently.

from_layout.description: Originating layout that was migrated. Only known historical value is 'docs+tdd' (the pre-workflow-cleanup layout); null means no migration has occurred.

offered_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when first-run preflight last offered to set up branch protection. Used to suppress repeated prompts.

applied_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when branch protection was successfully applied via gh api.

declined.description: True if user explicitly declined branch protection setup. Suppresses future prompts permanently until manually flipped.

profile.description: Named protection profile applied. Only known value today is 'team-pr-1approval-noforce' (PR required, 1 approval, no force-push).

ci.description: Continuous-integration posture for this repo. Read by the tp-pr-iterate loop's CI-settle wait (loop_driver._ci_settled_on_head).

expects_github_checks.description: Whether this repo expects GitHub-hosted check-runs on its PRs. Default (absent) is true — an empty statusCheckRollup is treated as 'CI not settled yet' (fail-closed). Set false when CI runs locally / off-GitHub (self-hosted-ci-runner): an empty rollup then counts as settled so the review loop converges instead of spinning to a cap. Only affects empty rollups; a non-empty rollup is evaluated normally regardless.

review.description: Code-review posture for this repo. Read by the tp-pr-iterate loop's two-stable success terminal (loop_driver._expects_copilot_review).

expects_copilot.description: Whether GitHub Copilot code review is an available reviewer on this repo's PRs. Default (absent) is true — the two-stable terminal requires copilot_reviewed_successfully as its third conjunct, so a Copilot-reviewed repo never converges on an absent/stale Copilot signal. Set false when Copilot code review is NOT attachable (e.g. an individual account with no Copilot entitlement — a structural absence, distinct from a transient 'Copilot encountered an error' review): the loop is already dual-source, so the /code-review arm then carries the terminal on its own (codereview-clean + zero unresolved actionable threads + the minor-only classifier flip) instead of spinning to cap-exhausted. Only DROPS the Copilot conjunct; the /code-review and ground-truth-thread conjuncts are unchanged, so convergence is never weakened into a false positive.

require_human_approval.description: Whether the deterministic merge gate requires a current human-approval (`tp:human-approved` on the PR head) as its fifth predicate. Default (absent) is true — the gate folds pred_human_approved strictly, so the autonomous path can never cross the irreversible `gh pr merge` boundary without an explicit, out-of-band human approval that is current on the head SHA. Set false only to OPT OUT of the human-approval predicate (the other four predicates still gate); the predicate is then OMITTED from evaluate_gate and the pre-change four-predicate fold is unchanged. IMPORTANT: setting false opts out of the gate predicate, but land.py still hard-refuses unattended landing at the land boundary — the opt-out never crosses the irreversible boundary without a human approving at HEAD. The flag is read from committed HEAD only (via `git show HEAD:`), so an uncommitted edit cannot change this value. The predicate is read by deterministic_gate.evaluate_gate via human_approval._require_human_approval. Deny-rule residual: the permissions.deny rule Bash(git commit --no-verify:*) uses a prefix match and cannot block '--no-verify' appearing after other arguments; prose prohibitions (CLAUDE.md, commit-after-work.md) and reviewer inspection remain the backstop for those forms.

automation_identities.description: Optional additional automation logins (lowercased) treated as NON-human by the human-approval predicate, beyond the hardcoded floor (github-actions[bot], dependabot[bot], the Copilot REST logins) and the framework's own resolved self-login. Use this for committing CI service accounts so an approval applied by such an account never satisfies pred_human_approved (separation-of-duties / multi-account SoD). Each entry is matched case-insensitively against the labeling actor's login. A non-list value is ignored gracefully.

pdw.description: parallel-design-worktrees configuration: loop guards, URL allowlist for fix-comments, and runner backend selector.

diff_growth_multiplier.description: Multiplier vs. original_diff_lines at which the loop transitions convergence-failure. Default 3.

k_consecutive.description: Number of consecutive structural-present rounds that triggers convergence-failure. Default 3.

idle_timeout_sec.description: Seconds with no new comments after a non-structural verdict before transitioning to awaiting-human-review. Default 1800.

comment_url_allowlist.description: Additional URL netlocs allowed in fix-comment bodies beyond github.com and the repo origin host.

type.description: Runner backend identifier. v1 supports 'claude' only — `run_supervisor._wrap_slash` raises NotImplementedError on any other value, so the schema fails the misconfig at validation time rather than at runtime. The `local-run-auto-sessions` design will broaden this enum.

fleet.description: Fleet-level configuration for worktree-isolation guards and related tunables.

diff_balloon_factor.description: Multiplier vs. base diff size at which the diff-balloon guard fires and rejects the candidate. Default 5.

worktree_immunization.description: Seat immunization state: tracks whether the heal-core-bare hook + extensions.worktreeConfig have been offered, applied, or declined. Mirrors the branch_protection block shape so the first-run preflight can check it with a single config-read condition.

offered_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when the immunization offer was last shown to the operator.

applied_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when immunization was successfully applied (heal hooks installed + extensions.worktreeConfig=true set). Null if not yet applied.

declined.description: True if the operator explicitly declined immunization. Permanently suppresses the offer so first-run never re-asks.
